ReportText:
PATHOLOGY REPORT - OMENTAL BIOPSY

SPECIMEN: Omental mass, CT-guided core biopsy (2 cores)

DIAGNOSIS: High-grade serous carcinoma, consistent with ovarian/fallopian tube/peritoneal primary.

MICROSCOPIC: Cores of fibroadipose tissue extensively infiltrated by a high-grade epithelial neoplasm arranged in papillary, solid, and slit-like glandular patterns. Marked nuclear atypia with prominent nucleoli and frequent mitotic figures (>12 per 10 HPF). Psammoma bodies present. No mucinous or clear cell components identified.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
- PAX8: Positive (diffuse, strong)
- WT1: Positive (diffuse, strong)
- p53: Aberrant pattern (diffuse strong overexpression, consistent with TP53 missense mutation)
- CK7: Positive
- CK20: Negative
- ER: Positive (80%, strong intensity)
- PR: Positive (10%, weak intensity)
- p16: Block positive (diffuse, strong)
- Napsin-A: Negative
- HER2: Negative (IHC 0)
- Ki-67: 70%
- MLH1: Intact
- MSH2: Intact
- MSH6: Intact
- PMS2: Intact

MOLECULAR TESTING:
- BRCA1: Pathogenic variant detected (c.5266dupC / 5382insC) - GERMLINE
- BRCA2: No pathogenic variant
- HRD Score (Myriad myChoice CDx): 58 (Positive; cutoff ≥42)
- MSI Status: Microsatellite Stable (MSS)
- PD-L1 (22C3): Combined Positive Score (CPS) = 8
- TMB: 4.2 mutations/Mb (low)
- No NTRK fusions detected
- Additional somatic variants: TP53 p.R248W (pathogenic), NF1 p.Q1336* (likely pathogenic)

COMMENT: Morphology and immunoprofile are diagnostic of high-grade serous carcinoma. The aberrant p53 staining pattern is consistent with the detected TP53 missense mutation. BRCA1 germline pathogenic variant confirmed - patient is eligible for PARP inhibitor therapy. HRD-positive status further supports platinum and PARP inhibitor sensitivity. Intact MMR excludes Lynch syndrome-associated endometrial/ovarian cancer. Recommend genetic counseling for the patient and at-risk family members.

PATHOLOGY REPORT - INTERVAL DEBULKING SURGERY

SPECIMENS:
A. Uterus with cervix and bilateral adnexa
B. Omentum
C. Right pelvic lymph nodes
D. Left pelvic lymph nodes
E. Para-aortic lymph nodes
F. Appendix
G. Right diaphragm peritoneal biopsy
H. Left diaphragm peritoneal biopsy
I. Right paracolic gutter biopsy
J. Left paracolic gutter biopsy
K. Cul-de-sac peritoneal biopsy
L. Bladder peritoneal biopsy

DIAGNOSIS:

A. UTERUS, CERVIX, AND BILATERAL ADNEXA:
- RIGHT OVARY: Residual high-grade serous carcinoma, 2.5 cm, with treatment effect (approximately 40% viable tumor, 60% fibrosis/necrosis). Ovarian surface involvement present.
- LEFT FALLOPIAN TUBE: High-grade serous carcinoma involving fimbrial end, 1.8 cm, with treatment effect (approximately 30% viable tumor). Serous tubal intraepithelial carcinoma (STIC) identified in the fimbrial mucosa - likely site of origin.
- LEFT OVARY: No residual carcinoma.
- UTERUS: Inactive endometrium. Benign leiomyoma (1.2 cm). No carcinoma.
- CERVIX: Benign. No dysplasia.

B. OMENTUM: Residual high-grade serous carcinoma, single focus 0.5 cm, with extensive treatment effect (>90% fibrosis).

C. RIGHT PELVIC LYMPH NODES: 0 of 12 lymph nodes positive for carcinoma.
D. LEFT PELVIC LYMPH NODES: 0 of 10 lymph nodes positive for carcinoma.
E. PARA-AORTIC LYMPH NODES: 1 of 8 lymph nodes positive for carcinoma (0.3 cm metastatic deposit with treatment effect). No extracapsular extension.

F. APPENDIX: No carcinoma. Benign.
G-L. PERITONEAL BIOPSIES (all sites): No residual carcinoma. Fibrosis consistent with treatment effect.

PATHOLOGIC STAGING (post-neoadjuvant):
- ypT3c: Tumor involves ovaries with peritoneal metastasis beyond pelvis (omentum) and retroperitoneal lymph node involvement
- ypN1: Regional lymph node metastasis (1 para-aortic node)
- ypM0: No distant metastasis
- FIGO Stage IIIC
- Chemotherapy Response Score (CRS): 2 (appreciable tumor response amid viable tumor readily identifiable)

TREATMENT EFFECT: Partial response with 40-60% treatment effect across specimens. R0 resection achieved (no gross residual disease).

SYNOPTIC SUMMARY:
- Primary site: Right ovary and left fallopian tube (fimbrial STIC identified)
- Histologic type: High-grade serous carcinoma
- Grade: High grade (Grade 3)
- LVSI: Present (focal)
- Margins: Negative
- Lymph nodes: 1/30 positive (para-aortic)
- Treatment effect: CRS 2
- Residual disease: R0
